debilities.name: diminished
debilities.effect: disadvantage to Deploy, Muster, or Pull Together

debilities.name: lacking
debilities.effect: treat Prosperity as if it's 1 lower than it is

debilities.name: malcontent
debilities.effect: Fortunes reset to +0 each season, not +1; folks need Persuading more often than usual

content.text:
Keep this in sync with the GM playbook. Review it at the start of each session.

When *anyone calls "time out,"* play stops. Step out of character, check in with each other, maybe take a break. Discuss what's wrong, player-to-player.

If *content was included that shouldn't have been*, acknowledge the mistake, fix the fiction, and move on.

If *someone realizes they need content to be excluded, veiled, or handled in a particular way*, then update the lists. Clarify specifics, now or later, but don't ask reasons. Fix the fiction. Check in with the player(s). When everyone is ready, move on.

lists.title: Excluded content
lists.note: Not part of the game, on-camera or off

lists.title: Veiled content
lists.note: Part of the fiction, but only off-camera

improvements.name: Additional Housing
improvements.summary: It's getting crowded! We need more room to live.

either: An *exceptional* engineer/foreman, to design much roomier houses on the current land
Building on parts of the fields, resulting in -1 Surplus generated with each autumn's harvest

andThen.text: Pulling together 5 times, each requiring 1 season, 1 Surplus, and a wagonload of timber and other supplies (Value 2), to (re)build homes.

improvements.effects:
When you *meet the requirements*, increase Fortunes by 1 and add any new homes to the map.

Henceforth, when *you consume Surplus in winter*, consider Population to be 1 lower than it is.

improvements.name: Aurochs Hunting
improvements.summary: Large herds form on the Flats in spring. The Hillfolk hunt them, but Stonetop has never learned to do so.

options: A Herd of Horses (and hunters to ride them)
Cooperating with the Hillfolk
A cunning plan

andThen.text: A successful first hunt (played out in detail)

improvements.effects:
When you *meet the requirements*, add "Aurochs hunting (meat, hide, horn)" to the Resources list.

Henceforth, when you *lead the aurochs hunt in spring*, roll +Defenses: **on a 10+**, gain 1d4 Surplus; **on a 7-9**, gain 1d4 Surplus but pick 1 from the list below; **on a 6-**, pick 1 from the list below, or pick 2 and gain 1d4 Surplus.

- 1d4 of the town's horses are lamed or killed
- A number of locals are injured; the steading marks *diminished* (disadvantage to Deploy, Muster, or Pull Together)
- The GM picks an NPC present for the hunt; they are killed
- The Hillfolk are somehow offended
- The herd is weak; if you hunt next year they'll be wiped out

improvements.name: Expanded Trades
improvements.summary: Specialization is the key to prosperity!

options: Harnessing the Stream
Raincatching
Mill
A chandler with extensive tools and supplies (Value 3)
A glassblower with a full glassworks (Value 3)
An *exceptional* weaver with good tools (Value 2) and a reliable supply of Whitefang wool
An *exceptional* potter with good tools (Value 2) and a reliable source of excellent clay
An *exceptional* smith with a newer, hotter forge (Value 3)
Some other *exceptional* tradesperson, with the appropriate tools and supplies (Value 2 or 3)

improvements.effects: When you *meet the requirements*, increase Prosperity by 1. If you *cease to meet the requirements*, decrease Prosperity by 1.

improvements.name: Greater Harvest
improvements.summary: Beyond the Old Wall, the prairie grass of the Flats chokes out any crops we try to grow.

options: Doubling the yield of crops inside the Old Wall
Clearing/taming new fields beyond the Old Wall

improvements.effects:
When you *meet the requirements*, increase Fortunes by 1.

Henceforth, when *the autumn harvest is complete*, gain +1d4 Surplus.

improvements.name: Harnessing the Stream
improvements.summary: A shallow creek flows just below the town. If only it could be harnessed!

options: A reservoir for the Stream to pool in, and some way for the water to flow uphill
A series of aqueducts, from the Stream's source back to Stonetop

improvements.effects:
When you *meet the requirements*, add it to the Resources list and increase Fortunes by 1.

Henceforth, *when spring breaks forth and you roll a 7+ with Fortunes*, the steading generates 1 Surplus.

improvements.name: Herd of Horses
improvements.summary: Imagine what we could do with a dozen fine steeds.

all: A site for a proper stable and corral
Pulling Together to build the stable and corral, which requires a month and a wagonload of timber (Value 2). Add them to the map.
Someone skilled in riding and training horses
Acquiring a small herd of horses, about a dozen (through trade or by catching wild ones)
Training/breaking them to the saddle and plow
Additional saddles, harness, plows, etc. (Value 2)
Pulling Together to have a couple dozen villagers learn to ride, requiring a season and 1 Surplus.
Someone to mind the herd and stable, full time

improvements.effects:
When you *meet the requirements*, increase Fortunes by 1 and replace "a pair of sturdy draft horses" with "a herd of horses" on the Assets list. Make a note of its size. Henceforth:

When you *leverage the horses to Pull Together*, it takes half as long and costs half as much.

When you *Requisition half the herd or less*, treat a 6- as a 7-9.

When the *Seasons Change to summer*, any yearlings become horses (Value 3 once trained), any foals become yearlings (Value 2), and the herd gains foals (Value 1) equal to 1d4+Fortunes (min 0).

When *winter grips the land*, the herd consumes 1 Surplus per 6 grown or yearling horses. For every Surplus not consumed, 1d6 horses are lost.

improvements.name: Heroic Reputation
improvements.summary: Few have heard of Stonetop's heroes. Yet.

options: Impressing a band of Hillfolk
Braving a lake and coming back with proof
Saving many Marshedge residents' lives
Saving many Gordin's Delve residents' lives
Saving someone from beyond Marshedge
Hiring a minstrel to tell your tales (Value 2)

improvements.effects:
When you *meet the requirements*, gain the following move:

When you *first meet someone from beyond Stonetop*, roll +Fortunes: **on a 10+,** say what they've heard about you or Stonetop, and gain advantage on your next move against them; **on a 7-9**, say what they've heard; **on a 6-**, the GM decides what they've heard.

improvements.name: Inn
improvements.summary: The public house offers a common room and shelter for a few horses, but it's hardly a proper inn.

all: A designated building site
A competent engineer/foreman
Furnishings, equipment, and material (Value 3)

all.text: Pulling Together 2 times, each requiring 1 season, 1 Surplus, and timber/supplies (Value 2)

all: A small, devoted staff (innkeep, cook, ostler, etc.)

improvements.effects:
When you *meet the requirements*, increase Fortunes by 1. Name the inn, add it to both the Resources list and map.

Henceforth, when *the seasons change*, whoever is friendliest rolls +Fortunes: **on a 10+**, ask the GM 3 questions about the wider world; **on a 7-9,** ask 1 question; **on a 6-**, ask 1 question, but the GM describes some trouble that stems from the inn or its guests.

Once per season, when you *expend 1 Surplus and bring folks together at the inn* (to talk, to celebrate, to recuperate), clear one of the steading's debilities.

improvements.name: Market
improvements.summary: Stonetop is at most an afterthought for traders in the region. We need to change that.

options: A compelling good/service, exclusive to Stonetop
Establishing some other reason to visit Stonetop (place of pilgrimage, etc.)

andThen.text: A dedicated market site (add it to the map)
A trusted arbiter, able to enforce their own rulings on matters of trade
Four seasons in operation without notable incidents of violence, banditry, theft, etc.

improvements.effects:
When you *meet the requirements*, increase Prosperity by 1. If you *cease to meet the requirements*, decrease Prosperity by 1.

When the *Seasons Change to spring, summer, or autumn and the market is active*, and Population is +1 or better, the Market generates 1 Surplus.

improvements.name: Mill
improvements.summary: We've got our pick of millstones. With a mill, we'd have better bread and more time for other crafts.

all: An *exceptional* engineer/foreman
A convenient, consistent power source (wind on a hill, a waterwheel, a Herd of Horses, magic, etc.)
A building site able to harness that power source

all.text: Pulling Together 2 times, each requiring a season, 1 Surplus, a wagonload of timber (Value 2), and a bunch of rope and supplies (Value 2)

all: A full-time miller

improvements.effects:
When you *meet the requirements*, increase Fortunes by 1, add "Mill" to the Resources list and draw it on the map.

Henceforth, when *the autumn harvest is complete*, the steading generates +1 Surplus. Also, when you *Outfit from Stonetop or Have What You Need after doing so*, each ◇ of supplies has 1 extra use.

improvements.name: Palisade
improvements.summary: A wall of sharpened logs, 10' tall, to keep evil at bay.

all: Lots of timber (~20-25 wagonloads, Value 3)
A competent engineer/foreman
Lots of rope, nails, pitch, etc. (Value 2)
Pulling Together, costing a month and 1 Surplus

improvements.effects:
When you *meet the requirements*, increase Fortunes by 1, add "Palisade" to the Fortifications list and draw it on the map.

Henceforth, when you *take advantage of the palisade*, you have advantage to Deploy.

improvements.name: Raincatching
improvements.summary: Filling the cistern takes so much work. Surely, we can do better!

all: An *exceptional* engineer/foreman, to design a cunning system of roofs, gutters, and conduits
Enough slate/terracotta to roof all the buildings and construct the gutters and conduits (Value 3)

all.text: Pulling Together 3 times, each requiring 1 season and 1 Surplus

improvements.effects: When you *meet the requirements*, increase Fortunes by 1, add "Raincatching" to the Resources list. Henceforth, when *summer comes and you roll a 7+ with Fortunes*, the steading generates 1 Surplus.

improvements.name: Standing Watch
improvements.summary: Some full-time warriors would make us all safer, no?

all: A veteran warrior, able to command a crowd
At least 6 warriors, well-equipped and willing
The village leaders agreeing to support warriors who train and keep watch full-time

improvements.effects:
When you *meet the requirements*, add "standing watch" to the Fortifications list. *At the start of each season*, the watch consumes 1 Surplus or it disbands.

When you *specifically involve the watch in a move*, treat Defenses as 1 higher than they are.

improvements.name: Stone Wall
improvements.summary: No mere palisade of wood, but a mighty rampart. We have the stone, after all...

all: An *exceptional* engineer/foreman
A stonecutter with an able crew
Equipment, tools, and material (Value 3)

all.text: Pulling Together 4 times, each costing 1 season, 1 Surplus, and supplies (Value 2)

improvements.effects:
When you *meet the requirements*, add "Stone Wall" to the Fortifications list (erase "Palisade" if you had it) and draw it on the map. Henceforth:

When you *take advantage of the stone wall*, you have advantage to Deploy.

When *winter grips the land*, the steading consumes 1 less Surplus than normal.

improvements.name: Township
improvements.summary: Will this ever be more than a backwater village?

all: Additional Housing
Raincatching OR Diverting the Stream
A formal government of some sort

improvements.effects:
When you *mark all of the requirements*, change Size to *town* and its Population to +0. Henceforth:

When you *Muster, Pull Together*, or *Trade & Barter*, you have advantage.

When *the seasons change to spring or summer*, the town generates Surplus equal to Population+1. But, when *winter grips the land*, roll 2d6+Population to consume Surplus instead of 1d4+Population.

improvements.name: Weapons of War
improvements.summary: Spears are great, but how about axes, picks, swords?

either: Acquiring a few dozen good swords, battleaxes, maces, flails, warhammers, etc. (Value 3)
orAll: A smith, with a full staff and upgraded tools (Value 2)
A cartload of good iron ore (Value 2)

orAll.text: 4 seasons of work by the smith

andThen.text: A veteran warrior, able to command a crowd
Pulling Together to train the militia with these new weapons, requiring a season and 1 Surplus

improvements.effects:
When you *meet the requirements*, increase Defenses by 1 and add "Weapons of War" to the Fortifications list. *Each spring*, the village must expend 1 Surplus to maintain and replace the town's weapons.

Henceforth, when you *Outfit from Stonetop or Have What You Need after doing so*, you can treat maces, flails, battleaxes, warhammers, and all types of swords as common items, as if they were already on the inventory inserts. Battleaxes and swords have "x piercing," where x is the steading's current Prosperity.

improvements.name: Well-Trained Militia
improvements.summary: Everyone can use a spear and shield, but some hard drilling could make us a force to be reckoned with.

options: A veteran warrior, able to command a crowd
requires.perTactic: For each tactic below, you must then Pull Together, requiring a season of drills and 1 Surplus.
tactics: Archery: barrages, ranged ambushes, sniping, etc.
Cavalry (requires a Herd of Horses): fighting from horseback, charges
Formations: shield walls, wedges, phalanx, etc.
Readiness: patrolling, reacting quickly to alarms

improvements.effects:
When you *Deploy using one of the militia's trained tactics*, you are likely acting from a position of strength (you pick the consequence on a 7-9, not the GM).

When the *militia has trained in 2+ tactics*, increase Defenses by 1.

*Each summer*, the militia must spend 1 Surplus and a week or so practicing or else lose its training in 1 tactic.

otherImprovements.text: Additional possible Improvements, revealed by the GM.

assets.text: Owned by the residents of Stonetop in common. To take them on an expedition or otherwise put them at risk, you must Requisition.
starting: A pair of hardy draft horses, followers (*large*, *powerful*, *keen-nosed*, *hardy*): HP 10 each; Damage d6+3 (*hand*, *close*, *forceful*); Instinct: to panic; Cost: care & grooming.
A pair of horse-drawn plows, iron
A pair of carts (plus horse harness)

residents.text: Add each NPC that is named during introductions or play. Give each an occupation (even if just farmer or homemaker) and at least 1 trait.
npcTraits: dallied with the Fae years ago
has Fae blood in their veins
has no respect for their elders
has their head in the clouds
swears they met the Pale Hunter

neighbors.text: Add NPCs from neighboring areas when instructed by a playbook, or as needed.

places.name: Gordin's Delve
places.namesNote: Choose from other lists; everyone comes to Gordin's Delve from somewhere else.
